Reid's Unzipped

Voyage Log

Chronicles from 15,577 nautical miles and counting — passages, anchorages, adventures ashore, and everything in between.

Follow Zipper in real time
Filter:

Video Highlights

Watch our videos from the Atlantic crossing, Pacific passages and everywhere in between — all in one place.

YouTube playlist - "Zipper"
📍 Whangarei, New Zealand
28 Nov 2025 Jamie Reid
A year in Whangarei?!

An unplanned year in Whangarei — dealing with a stroke, skin cancer and a broken arm, we feel we've tested the NZ health service sufficiently. It's time to go sailing again.

cruising whangarei zipper
Read →
📍 Whangarei, New Zealand
6 Nov 2024 Jamie Reid
Cherbourg to Whangarei: 15,577Nm

15,577 miles since leaving France, we have arrived in New Zealand — and we brought three Kiwis home with us.

cruising new-zealand passage
Read →
📍 Makatea, French Polynesia
10 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Video Highlights – Makatea

Some highlights of our stay in Makatea, exploring the island with the irrepressible Tapu.

caves makatea zipper
Read →
📍 Makatea, French Polynesia
10 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Makatea – mined, abandoned and reborn

A small island between Tahiti and the Tuamotus, Makatea is unlike anywhere else — pushed up from the ocean, mined for phosphate, and now quietly coming back to life.

makatea history cruising
Read →
📍 Makatea, French Polynesia
8 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Arrival in Makatea

We were just starting to feel a bit sad as we left the Tuamotus behind — we needn't have worried. Makatea is spectacular.

makatea cruising sailing
Read →
📍 Tuamotus, French Polynesia
7 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Video Highlights – The Tuamotus

What an amazing month in the most spectacular coral atolls in the middle of the Pacific.

tuamotus video cruising
Read →
📍 Toau, French Polynesia
7 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Enjoying Toau – Anse Amyot

Our last atoll in the Tuamotus, and it did not disappoint. Lobster, spearfishing, diving, and the warmth of Gaston and Valentine's hospitality.

toau tuamotus cruising
Read →
📍 Tuamotus, French Polynesia
4 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Tuamotus Passes

Coming out of the North pass at Fakarava, it felt like time to write down our experience of the various atoll passes before we forget. Some were textbook, some were anything but.

cruising passes tuamotus
Read →
📍 Fakarava, French Polynesia
4 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Fakarava (aka "Sharkarava")

Sharks, sharks and more sharks. Fakarava's South Pass is one of the great dive sites in the world — and we had over 500 of them right in front of us.

cruising diving fakarava
Read →
📍 Fakarava, French Polynesia
1 Aug 2024 Jamie Reid
Front, Wind Shift, Anchor Drag

A 180-degree wind shift as a front went overhead, and our anchor didn't reset. A textbook example of a Rocna failure mode — and a lesson about bimini sides in strong winds.

anchor anchoring cruising
Read →
📍 Kauehi, French Polynesia
27 Jul 2024 Jamie Reid
Chilling Out in Kauehi

Visiting Tearavero and a few days relaxing on a secluded motu in the East of Kauehi — shell beaches, hermit crabs, bonfires, and a perfect drift snorkel out of the pass.

kauehi tuamotus cruising
Read →
📍 Tahanea, French Polynesia
21 Jul 2024 Jamie Reid
Finding Shelter in Tahanea

Hiding out from a weather front in the South of Tahanea after snorkelling the passes in the North — coral bleaching? Not here. The Tuamotus are spectacular.

tahanea tuamotus cruising
Read →
📍 Tuamotus, French Polynesia
20 Jul 2024 Jamie Reid
Video Highlights – First Few Days in the Tuamotus

Highlights is a bit of a misnomer really — the Tuamotus are mostly about what's going on under the water. Anyway, you'll get the gist.

tuamotus video cruising
Read →
📍 Raroia, French Polynesia
13 Jul 2024 Jamie Reid
The Tuamotus – Landfall in Raroia

Our first atoll, and what an introduction. Octopus, paddle boarding firsts, the Kon Tiki monument, and an unofficial yacht club on the beach.

cruising octopus paddle-boarding
Read →
📍 Passage
9 Jul 2024 Jamie Reid
Marquesas to Tuamotus

Timing is everything, especially when you are dependent on the weather. The predicament for this 450Nm passage — leave too early and it will be very rough, too late and the wind dies.

passage tuamotus marquesas
Read →
📍 Hiva Oa, Marquesas
19 Jun 2024 Jamie Reid
Arriving in the Marquesas

Zipper's arrival into Hiva Oa — checking in to French Polynesia, the bureaucratic workaround everyone uses, and a drive around one of the world's most spectacular islands.

marquesas hiva-oa cruising
Read →
📍 Panama
28 Apr 2024 Sophie Reid
Panama and Preparing for the Canal

Arriving in Panama, preparing for the canal, provisioning for the Pacific — and a gooseneck pin that nearly ruined everything.

panama canal cruising
Read →
📍 Tobago Cays, Grenadines
6 Apr 2024 Jamie Reid
Tobago Cays

Time and again people told us Tobago Cays was their favourite place in the Caribbean. It certainly didn't disappoint.

caribbean rays tobago-cays
Read →
📍 St. Lucia, Caribbean
4 Apr 2024 Jamie Reid
St Lucia

From a surprise tuna for supper on the crossing to sleeping between the Pitons — a glorious few days in St. Lucia that got everyone on board smiling again.

cruising mud-baths pitons
Read →
📍 Martinique, Caribbean
3 Apr 2024 Jamie Reid
Between the boat work jobs

It's not all spanners and epoxy — in between the jobs list we found time to snorkel, meet our sister ships, and celebrate a couple of very important birthdays.

cruising martinique snorkelling
Read →
📍 Bequia, St. Vincent and the Grenadines
2 Apr 2024 Jamie Reid
Beautiful Bequia

A brief but brilliant stop in Bequia — dolphin escort, a new speed record of 14.5 knots, turtles, and a chance meeting with fellow cruisers Stuart and Anne on Time Bandit.

sailing cruising bequia
Read →
📍 Marin, Martinique
23 Mar 2024 Jamie Reid
Scope creep

We came in for a broken shroud and left with completely new standing rigging. If you're going to find a serious rig problem, at least do it at the best rigging yard around.

cruising boat-work rigging
Read →
📍 Marin, Martinique
19 Mar 2024 Jamie Reid
Boat work in Marin

Rudders, saildrive seals, a mast that needed to come out, a waterline that needed raising — two weeks in the yard in Marin and Zipper got a proper overhaul.

antifoul boat-work boatwork
Read →
📍 Martinique, Caribbean
23 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Martinique – back where it all began

We bought Zipper in Martinique, and now we've sailed her all the way back — though our arrival was rather more dramatic than we'd have liked, courtesy of a stray fishing line and a stalled engine.

cruising lobster-pots martinique
Read →
📍 Barbados, Caribbean
20 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Checked out ready to leave, thank you Barbados

A last beer and swim in the pool, then we're off — Barbados has been wonderful, and we're pointing Zipper at Martinique before first light.

cruising barbados sailing
Read →
📍 Barbados, Caribbean
19 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Barbados marine parks and lifeguards

Snorkelling with turtles straight off the back of the boat, a run-in with some particularly unhelpful lifeguards, and an unexpected carburettor lesson in the chop behind the boat.

barbados carburettor cruising
Read →
📍 Barbados, Caribbean
16 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Official cruiser status

9 months since we bought the boat, 5,000 miles sailed, and finally — snorkelling with turtles before boat-school on a Monday morning — we feel like proper cruisers at last.

barbados caribbean cruising
Read →
📍 Passage, Atlantic
14 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Dolphin

From phosphorescent torpedo-run arrivals in the night to pods that stayed with us for half an hour — the Atlantic was generous with its dolphin, and we were lucky enough to witness every one.

atlantic cruising dolphin
Read →
📍 Mindelo, Cape Verde
9 Feb 2024 Jamie Reid
Pit stop – Mindelo, Cape Verde

A grinding autopilot, a week of waiting, broken springs, misaligned rudders, and Gilson working through the weekend — Mindelo was never part of the plan, but we're very glad it was there.

autopilot boatcv cruising
Read →
📍 Lanzarote, Canary Islands
4 Jan 2024 Jamie Reid
Extending our 90-day Schengen allowance

We broke the boat and couldn't leave Spain — which meant five trips to the police station, a 58-euro taxi, a mobile printer, and one of the more memorable bureaucratic adventures of our lives.

cruising lanzarote schengen
Read →
📍 Lanzarote, Canary Islands
4 Jan 2024 Jamie Reid
Boatlife – daily surprises

One new boat problem per day, every day over Christmas — from a leaking epoxy hardener to a chart card that gave up the ghost. Boatlife keeps you humble.

cruising boat-work canary-islands
Read →
📍 Passage, Atlantic
6 Oct 2023 Jamie Reid
The Question

To go, or not to go? The answer was always yes — the real question was when. Here's how we got to the starting line.

sailing cruising family
Read →
📍 En route to Martinique
6 Oct 2023 Jamie Reid
Now we're committed!

Written on the plane to Martinique to take ownership of Zipper — excited, anxious, and about to find out exactly how naïve we are.

sailing cruising family
Read →
